Remove redundant CTRF conversion and Node.js setup

Catch2 listener writes ctrf-report.json directly — no need for
npx junit-to-ctrf conversion step or Node.js. Reporter reads
ctrf-report.json at the root, not ctrf/*.json glob.

* Expand conditional glvalue fix plan

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/181003b9-5b11-46a4-a2cd-1a35b014f29d

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add conditional glvalue regression tests

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/181003b9-5b11-46a4-a2cd-1a35b014f29d

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix conditional glvalue address regressions

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/181003b9-5b11-46a4-a2cd-1a35b014f29d

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review comments

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/GregorGullwi/FlashCpp/sessions/ae7a938c-08fa-4d90-8237-7d147b5fde6d

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix comment indentation to match code level (2 tabs, not 3)

* Fix ternary branch assignment RHS to preserve pointer_depth and other TypedValue metadata

In the non-LValueAddress path, the branch assignment RHS was using
makeTypedValue() which only sets type_index, size_in_bits, and value,
losing pointer_depth, ir_type, and ref_qualifier. This could cause
downstream IR lowering to misinterpret pointer values (e.g.,
`cond ? ptr1 : ptr2` would have pointer_depth=0 instead of 1).

Restore the original toTypedValue() behavior for the RHS in the normal
(non-address) path, while keeping the LValueAddress path unchanged.
Split the single makeMergedBranchValue lambda into separate makeBranchLhs
and makeBranchRhs helpers for clarity.

* Remove fixed known issue: ternary member access in address-of context

This issue was fixed in this PR by propagating ExpressionContext into
generateTernaryOperatorIr and materializing addresses in each branch.
The entry was accidentally re-added during a rebase.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: devin-ai-integration[bot] <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Gregor Gullwi <gregor.gullwi@gmail.com>

commit.message:
CI uses build.sh and runs tests via binary directly

- Created build.sh: cmake configure + build, auto-detects vcpkg
  and parallel jobs (nproc/sysctl)
- CI build step: ./build.sh (not inline cmake commands)
- CI test step: ./build/shapeshifter_tests (not ctest)
  Produces ctrf-report.json directly via Catch2 listener
- run.sh delegates to build.sh
- Windows CI: bash shell for build.sh compatibility

commit.message:
fix(auth): prevent forced logout loop on backend 401

Retry once after token refresh, and only dispatch session-expired when no active Supabase session exists. This stops immediate redirect back to login when backend returns 401 despite a valid client session.

commit.message:
fix: 'finishing transcript' stuck after stop

- _after_engine_stopped now force-resets transcript phase state:
  clears _ignore_transcript_phase_updates, cancels pending decode
  finish callbacks, hides progress bar, sets 'idle'
- Drain loop capped with max_iters to prevent runaway looping
- Ensures UI always recovers to clean state after engine stops

Step 2: Generated bridge crate bootstrap and bridge registry integration (#32)

* feat(bridge): scaffold qmlts-host-generated crate with BridgeInstance/BridgeDescriptor

Add the generated bridge crate to the workspace. It provides:
- BridgeInstance: type-erased owner of (vm, runtime) QObject pairs
- BridgeDescriptor: static class_name + factory registration
- descriptors(): returns all registered bridge types (empty for now)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(bridge): add LoginViewModel, CounterViewModel, LoginRuntime, CounterRuntime bridge QObjects

LoginViewModel: username (QString), password (QString), isLoading (bool)
CounterViewModel: count (i32)
LoginRuntime: invoke(QVariant), onMounted(), onUnmounting(), onLoginCompleted(bool) signal
CounterRuntime: invoke(QVariant)

All properties match compiler golden schemas. Runtime QObjects use
#[auto_cxx_name] for proper camelCase QML access.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(generated): wire descriptors() with C++ factory functions

Add C++ factory functions (cpp/factories.cpp) that create QObject
instances via their generated constructors. Declare extern C FFI
in Rust and implement create_login_instance/create_counter_instance
factory functions. QObjectPair handles cleanup on drop.

Wire descriptors() to return LoginViewModel and CounterViewModel
descriptors with their factory functions.

Fix all clippy pedantic warnings (doc_markdown, must_use).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(host): add BridgeTypeNotFound and BridgeAlreadyLoaded error variants

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(host): add BridgeRegistry with qmlts-host-generated integration

BridgeRegistry maps ViewModel class names to factory functions from
qmlts-host-generated::descriptors(). Provides create_instance(),
has_type(), and registered_types() methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(host): add Qt context C++ shim and Rust FFI

Add cpp/qt_context.cpp with extern C wrappers for QQmlContext
setContextProperty/clear operations. Rust FFI in qt_context.rs
with safe wrappers and mock implementations for testing.

Update build.rs to compile C++ shim via CxxQtBuilder when Qt
is available (non-mock-qt).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(host): enhance QmltsEngine with bridge registry integration

Add BridgeRegistry, active_bridge, and qml_loaded fields to engine.
Add register_view_model(), has_bridge_type(), get_registered_types(),
and active_bridge() methods. Registration blocked after QML load.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(host): add napi exports for registerViewModel, getRegisteredTypes, hasBridgeType

Three new napi exports for bridge registry integration:
- registerViewModel(engine, className) - creates bridge QObject pair
- getRegisteredTypes(engine) - returns sorted list of available types
- hasBridgeType(engine, className) - checks if type is registered

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(host): add TypeScript declarations and tests for bridge registry

Add registerViewModel, getRegisteredTypes, hasBridgeType to:
- src/types.ts (documented declarations)
- index.d.ts (published declarations)
- src/index.ts (re-exports from native module)

Add 4 new bridge registry tests (TB-14 through TB-17):
- TB-14: getRegisteredTypes returns known types
- TB-15: hasBridgeType returns correct boolean
- TB-16: registerViewModel throws for unknown type
- TB-17: registerViewModel throws after QML loaded

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(bridge): add integration tests for generated crate and host

Generated crate tests (5): descriptor lookup, QObject instantiation,
valid non-null pointers, independent instances, clean drop.

Host integration tests (8): engine with registry, registered types,
register known types, unknown type error, register-after-load,
register-then-load QML, bridge pointer validity.

Also adds rlib to host crate-type for integration test support.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add real-Qt integration test step with QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen

Runs cargo test without --all-features (no mock-qt) to exercise
real cxx-qt bridge types against the installed Qt 6.11.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: fix Rust formatting and clippy warnings

Apply cargo fmt, fix unused import (CString behind cfg), add
allow(dead_code) on qt_context functions not yet called.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): decouple Rust tests from node-api linking

* fix(test): gate mock qt-context tests by feature

* fix(host): wire qt bridge context and harden review fixes

* fix(ci): repeat qt libs after shim archive

* fix(ci): run bun tests with offscreen qt

- Add mountedCount property to LoginRuntime for lifecycle observability
- Make onMounted() increment mountedCount (no longer a no-op)
- Add golden LoginView full-contract integration test (all 5 bindings)
- Add golden CounterView contract integration test
- Add actual golden LoginView.qml file loading test

- Add effect signal connection naming contract test
- Add invoke/mounted independence assertion test
- Add activeRuntimeI32Property napi export for behavioral TS testing
- Strengthen TB-17 with full golden bindings and observable assertions
- Update TypeScript declarations with new export

commit.message:
feat(mempalace): add KG, diary structure, and init artifacts

- mempalace/knowledge_graph.sqlite3: 251 triples seeded from program-status.yaml
  (has_status, belongs_to_phase, depends_on per petition; 99 entities)
- petitions/diaries/: agent diary directory for code-reviewer-strict and solution-architect
  (see README.md for format spec and recall commands)
- scripts/kg-seed-from-program-status.py: seed/reseed KG from program-status.yaml
- mempalace.yaml + entities.json: mempalace init artifacts (room config, detected entities)

Agents write diaries to petitions/diaries/<agent>/<petition>.md and emit triples via:
  python -m mempalace kg --kg ./mempalace/knowledge_graph.sqlite3 add ...

commit.message:
Add performance benchmarks (Catch2 BENCHMARK)

12 benchmarks covering every system at multiple entity counts:
- scroll: 10/100/1000 entities (2us → 172us)
- burnout: 1/10/100 obstacles (1.6us → 24us)
- collision: 1/10 obstacles (2us → 4us)
- scoring, gesture, player, lifetime, particle, cleanup
- full frame typical (6 obs + 20 particles): 27us
- full frame stress (50 obs + 50 particles): 96us

Full frame at 96us = 0.6% of 16ms budget. Plenty of headroom.

Run: ./run.sh bench

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Function constructor: ToString ordering, eval/arguments params, strict+with

- Fix ToString ordering: params are now ToString'd left-to-right before body
  per CreateDynamicFunction spec steps 7-8 (fixes S15.3.2.1_A3_T1/T3)
- Allow eval/arguments as parameter names in non-strict dynamic functions
  by using parse_without_strict_binding_checks during validation when body
  doesn't contain 'use strict' (fixes 15.3.2.1-11-3/4-s/7-s)
- Reject 'with' statements when body contains 'use strict' directive
  (fixes StrictFunction_reservedwords_with)
- Add run_vm_snippet_local_relaxed() helper for non-strict validation

Cluster 32: 630 pass (+6), 23 fail (16 FinalizationRegistry, 5 cross-realm, 2 other)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add build versioning (major.minor.githash)

Version generated at CMake configure time via version.h.in template.
Format: SHAPESHIFTER_VERSION = "0.1.<git-short-hash>"
Displayed in window title and logged at startup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix calendar data source and loading error states

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix ALL-CAPS rule: allow short acronyms, drop abbreviation check

- ALL-CAPS now only rejects words 5+ letters (GOLF, SAMUEL, BENEFITS)
- Short acronyms pass through: CIA, NBA, BBC, PBS, NFL, HIV, LSD, etc.
- Removed separate short-abbreviation rule that was blocking 'A' and 'I'
  as false abbreviations (e.g. 'A Holocaust Survivor', 'James I')
- Names with initials now pass: H.G. Wells, Booker T. Washington, etc.
  (spaCy POS handles these correctly as PROPN)
- Extracted shared _is_all_caps_noise() helper used by all 3 validators

Pool: 4,632 list / 1,135 action / 2,809 of-object

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: fix release and performance workflows

- Dockerfile: Install all deps (including devDeps) for TypeScript build, then prune for production
- release.yml: Make Docker job continue-on-error, decouple notify from Docker success
- performance.yml: Create benchmark/load-test scripts inside packages/mcp-server so require() resolves correctly (was creating at repo root but running from package dir)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
